Challenge 4: Sidorenko Conjecture for Bipartite Graphs with Bounded Sides

Definition 1 (Graph homomorphism density).

Let G and H be simple graphs.

  1. 1.

    A function f:V⁢(H)→V⁢(G) is a graph homomorphism from H to G if for each edge u⁢v∈E⁢(H), we have f⁢(u)⁢f⁢(v)∈E⁢(G).

  2. 2.

    Let hom⁢(H,G) be the number of graph homomorphisms H→G.

  3. 3.

    The homomorphism density of H in G is the fraction

    tH⁢(G):=hom⁢(H,G)|V⁢(G)||V⁢(H)|
Conjecture 1.1 (Sidorenko’s Conjecture [1]).

For every bipartite graph H and for any graph G, we have:

tH⁢(G)≥tK2⁢(G)|E⁢(H)|

We are interested in the special case of the conjecture where H is a bipartite graph in which each side has at most r vertices.

Open Problem 1.2 (Scaled Sidorenko’s Conjecture).

Let r∈ℕ. For every bipartite graph H that admits a bipartition V⁢(H)=X∪Y with |X|≤r and |Y|≤r and for any graph G, we have:

tH⁢(G)≥tK2⁢(G)|E⁢(H)|

References

  • [1] A. F. Sidorenko (1993) A correlation inequality for bipartite graphs. Graphs and Combinatorics 9 (2–4), pp. 201–204. doi: 10.1007/BF02988307.
